task_completion.description: Create a WFS-T XML request targeting the correct layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing XML content with <wfs:Transaction> and <wfs:Update typeName="ne:ne_populated_places"> (or similar prefix depending on workspace).

task_completion.description: Set the correct target attribute, value, and filter in the WFS-T request
task_completion.visual_evidence: The XML content must show <wfs:Property> with <wfs:Name>pop_max</wfs:Name> and <wfs:Value>28543210</wfs:Value>, along with an <ogc:Filter> (or similar) targeting the city 'Shanghai'.

task_completion.description: Save the WFS-T request XML to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal command (e.g., nano, vim, cat >) or file manager showing the XML file is saved exactly as /home/ga/wfs_update.xml.

task_completion.description: Send the WFS-T request to the GeoServer interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal showing a curl command (or similar HTTP client) sending a POST request with the XML file to the GeoServer WFS endpoint (e.g., http://localhost:8080/geoserver/wfs).

task_completion.description: Save the GeoServer response to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The curl command includes an output flag (e.g., -o /home/ga/wfs_response.xml or > /home/ga/wfs_response.xml), or a file listing shows /home/ga/wfs_response.xml was created.
Terminal output or a text editor showing the contents of /home/ga/wfs_response.xml containing a success indicator, such as <wfs:TransactionSummary> with totalUpdated="1".

integrity.description: The agent actually communicated with GeoServer rather than mocking the response
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a curl command or HTTP client execution sending the request. The response file should not be manually typed out in a text editor or created via a simple echo command.

integrity.description: The agent used WFS-T as requested, not direct database manipulation
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no psql commands or pgAdmin interactions running SQL UPDATE queries directly on the ne_populated_places table.

integrity.description: The response XML appears to be a genuine GeoServer output
integrity.visual_evidence: The contents of wfs_response.xml should contain standard GeoServer XML namespaces (e.g., xmlns:wfs, xmlns:ogc) and standard transaction response formatting, not a generic or simplified success string.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target city: 'Shanghai'. Target attribute: 'pop_max'. Target value: 28543210. Layer name: 'ne:ne_populated_places'. Request artifact path: '/home/ga/wfs_update.xml'. Response artifact path: '/home/ga/wfs_response.xml'. A successful WFS-T response from GeoServer typically includes a <wfs:TransactionResponse> tag with a <wfs:TransactionSummary> showing totalUpdated='1'.
